name: Consumer Adoption of Memory Cards: A Qualitative Study
description: Archival data package for the Aberdeen Group qualitative study on consumer adoption of SD memory cards, commissioned by the Internet Home Alliance (IHA) in July 2003. Based on 6 focus groups and 11 in-depth interviews in Boston and San Diego, examining consumer attitudes toward SD cards, transfer behaviors, purchase intent, and retail expectations.

about.name: SD Memory Card
about.description: Secure Digital removable flash memory card standard

about.name: Consumer Electronics Adoption
about.description: Consumer behavior and adoption patterns for digital electronics

about.name: Memory Card Format War
about.description: Competition between SD, CompactFlash, Memory Stick, and xD card formats

variableMeasured.name: Memory card brand awareness
variableMeasured.description: Consumer awareness and ability to identify memory card brands

variableMeasured.name: SD card purchase intent
variableMeasured.description: Consumer willingness and intent to purchase SD memory cards

variableMeasured.name: Premium willingness
variableMeasured.description: Willingness to pay premium for SD card capability in devices

variableMeasured.name: Preferred card capacity
variableMeasured.description: Consumer preference for SD card storage capacity

measurementTechnique: Qualitative research: 6 focus groups (3 Boston, 3 San Diego) and 11 in-depth interviews with parent/child dyads or triads
